task_completion.description: Verify that a sample house project is loaded in the software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main application window (DreamPlan Home Design Software) is open and displays a house model in the viewport, rather than an empty grid or start screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the ground floor (Story 1) is selected for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The UI shows 'Story 1', 'Ground Floor', or 'Level 1' highlighted or selected in the building/story management panel or toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify the ceiling height value has been changed to the target value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A settings panel, properties dialog, or input field specifically for 'Ceiling Height' displays the value '10' (if in feet), '120' (if in inches), or '3.05'/'3.048' (if in meters).

task_completion.description: Verify the final state shows the 3D view of the house.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport is set to a 3D perspective mode (showing volume and depth of the house) rather than a 2D top-down blueprint or floor plan view.

task_completion.description: Verify the new ceiling height is applied to the 3D model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view is clearly visible without blocking modal dialogs, and the ground floor walls/ceiling reflect the updated height (or the settings panel confirms the applied state while the 3D view is active).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the software's menus, toolbars, and input fields, rather than terminal windows executing background scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual home design software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to a legitimate 3D home design application (DreamPlan), not a text editor modifying project files or an image editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answer into an unrelated text field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value '10', '120', or '3.05' is entered into the specific 'Ceiling Height' property field, not a random search bar, chat window, or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target ceiling height is exactly 10 feet. Depending on the software's active unit settings, this must appear as 10 (feet), 120 (inches), or 3.048 / 3.05 (meters). Accept any of these mathematically equivalent values in the ceiling height input field. The software being used is DreamPlan Home Design Software.
